declarations.description:
Displays contextual feedback messages with semantic styling. Uses ARIA live regions for screen reader announcements.

Use `info` for neutral information, `success` for confirmations, `warning` for caution, `danger` for errors,
and `emergency` for critical system-wide alerts. Set `dismissible` to let users close the alert.
declarations.name: NysAlert

slots.description: Default slot for custom body content. Overrides `text` prop when provided.

members.name: id
members.description: Unique identifier. Auto-generated if not provided.

members.name: heading
members.description: Bold heading text displayed at the top of the alert.

members.name: icon
members.description: Custom icon name. Defaults to type-appropriate icon if not set.

members.name: dismissible
members.description: Shows close button allowing users to dismiss the alert.

members.name: duration
members.description: Auto-dismiss after specified milliseconds. Set to 0 to disable.

members.name: text
members.description: Body text content. Ignored if slot content is provided.

members.name: primaryAction
members.description: URL for the primary action link.

members.name: secondaryAction
members.description: URL for the secondary action link.

members.name: primaryLabel
members.description: Label text for primary action link.

members.name: secondaryLabel
members.description: Label text for secondary action link.

members.name: type
members.description: Semantic alert type affecting color and ARIA role. `danger`/`emergency` use assertive live region.

members.name: ariaAttributes
members.description:
Returns ARIA role and label based on alert type.
- 'alert' => assertive live region (implied)
- 'status' => polite live region
- 'region' => generic, requires aria-label

members.name: liveRegion
members.description:
Returns live-region type for screen readers if applicable.
- 'polite' for status role
- undefined for alert (since it's implicitly assertive) or region

members.name: _checkSlotContent
members.description:
Checks whether the default slot has content.
Updates `_slotHasContent` accordingly.

events.name: nys-close
events.description: Fired when alert is dismissed. Detail: `{id, type, label}`.

declarations.description:
A button for actions like saving, submitting, or navigating. Form-associated with full keyboard support.

Use `filled` for primary actions (one per section), `outline` for secondary, `ghost` for tertiary,
`text` for inline. Set `href` to render as a navigation link.
declarations.name: NysButton

cssProperties.description: Text color of the button label.
cssProperties.name: --nys-button-color

slots.description: Icon before label. Not shown for `text` variant.
slots.name: prefix-icon

slots.description: Icon after label. Not shown for `text` variant.
slots.name: suffix-icon

slots.description: Icon for circle mode. Overrides `icon` prop.

members.name: id
members.description: Unique identifier. Auto-generated if not provided.

members.name: name
members.description: Name for form submission.

members.name: size
members.description: Button height: `sm` (40px) for dense UIs, `md` (48px, default) for standard use, `lg` (56px) for prominent CTAs.

members.name: fullWidth
members.description: Expands button to fill container width. Use for mobile layouts or stacked button groups.

members.name: variant
members.description: Visual style: `filled` for primary (one per section), `outline` for secondary, `ghost` for tertiary, `text` for inline actions. Avoid `text` for navigation.

members.name: inverted
members.description: Adjusts colors for dark backgrounds.

members.name: label
members.description: Visible button text. Use sentence case, action-oriented text (e.g., "Save Draft"). Becomes aria-label in `circle` mode.

members.name: ariaLabel
members.description: Screen reader label. Required for icon-only buttons if `label` is not set.

members.name: ariaControls
members.description: ID of controlled element (e.g., dropdown or modal). Sets `aria-controls`.

members.name: tabIndex
members.description: Tab index for keyboard navigation. Defaults to 0 (focusable) when not disabled. Set to -1 to make focusable but not tabbable.

members.name: prefixIcon
members.description: Material Symbol icon before label. Not shown for `text` variant or `circle` mode.

members.name: suffixIcon
members.description: Material Symbol icon after label. Use `chevron_down` for dropdowns, `open_in_new` for external links. Not shown for `text` variant or `circle` mode.

members.name: circle
members.description: Renders circular icon-only button. Requires `icon` prop. `label` becomes aria-label.

members.name: icon
members.description: Icon for circle mode. Required when `circle` is true. Scales with size (sm=24px, md=32px, lg=40px).

members.name: disabled
members.description: Prevents interaction. Avoid disabling without explanation—show validation errors instead.

members.name: form
members.description: Form `id` to associate with. Use when button is outside the form element.

members.name: value
members.description: Value submitted with form data. Only used when `type="submit"`.

members.name: ariaDescription
members.description: Additional screen reader description. Sets `aria-description`.

members.name: type
members.description: Form behavior: `button` (default, no form action), `submit` (submits form), `reset` (resets form). Always set explicitly to avoid unintended submissions.

members.name: onClick
members.description: Click handler. Use instead of `@click` to ensure keyboard accessibility.

members.name: href
members.description: URL to navigate to. Renders as `<a>` tag. Omit for action buttons.

members.name: target
members.description: Link target: `_self` (same tab), `_blank` (new tab—add `suffixIcon="open_in_new"`), `_parent`, `_top`, or frame name.

members.name: _handleAnyAttributeFunction
members.description:
A Solution to the Vanilla JS & Native HTML keydown:

Handles inline onClick attributes set as strings in vanilla HTML
(e.g. <nys-button onClick="doSomething()">).

When onClick is set this way, it is a DOM attribute (not a property)
so this.onClick remains null. Native clicks execute the attribute
automatically, but keydown events do not, so we invoke it manually here.

events.name: nys-focus
events.description: Fired when the button receives focus.

events.name: nys-blur
events.description: Fired when the button loses focus.

events.name: nys-click
events.description: Fired when the button is clicked (mouse or keyboard). Not fired when disabled.

declarations.description:
A checkbox input for binary choices or multi-select lists. Can be used standalone or in a `nys-checkboxgroup`.
Form-associated with validation via ElementInternals.

Use for binary decisions (agree/disagree) or selecting multiple options from a list.
For single selection from 2-6 options, use `nys-radiobutton`. For immediate state changes, use `nys-toggle`.

declarations.description:
A container for grouping multiple `nys-checkbox` components as a single form control.
Handles validation, required constraints, and submits comma-separated values.

Use to allow users to select multiple options from a list. Apply `tile`, `size`, and `inverted` to the group
and all children inherit these styles automatically.

declarations.description:
`<nys-combobox>` is a form-enabled combo box combining text input with a filterable dropdown.

Features:
- Type to filter options
- Keyboard navigation (Arrow keys, Enter, Escape)
- Mouse and keyboard interaction
- Clears non-selected text on blur
- Clear button when value is selected
- Integrates with forms via ElementInternals
- Supports native <option> and <optgroup> elements
- Accessible per W3C ARIA Authoring Practices

members.name: _setValue
members.description:
Form helper methods:
- _setValue: set internal value and trigger validation
- _manageRequire: handle required state
- _validate: actively validate and show errors
- checkValidity: passive boolean check without UI
- _setValidityMessage: sync validation message with UI and internals
- _handleInvalid: handle form invalid event and focus first invalid field

members.name: _validate
members.description:
Actively validates the component:
- Updates internal validity state
- Updates UI (e.g. showError)
- Called on blur/change or form submission

members.name: checkValidity
members.description:
Passive check of validity:
- Returns true/false
- Does NOT update UI or show errors
- Used in form submission checks

members.name: _replaceButtonSVG
members.description:
Replaces the default wc-datepicker month navigation buttons
with NYS icon components for previous and next month.

members.name: _isMobile
members.description:
Determines whether the current device uses a coarse pointer.
A coarse pointer usually means touch-based input where precise pointing
is not expected, such as fingers on phones and most tablets.

Note: This is not a guarantee of a mobile device.
Some non-mobile devices may also report a coarse pointer,
and some mobile devices may not.

events.description: Fired on date selection. Detail: `{id, value}`.

events.description: Fired when input or calendar loses focus. Triggers validation.

Dismisses via close button or Escape key unless `mandatory` is set. Focus returns to trigger on close.

declarations.description:
A dropdown for selecting a single option from a list. Supports native `<option>` and `<optgroup>` elements.
Form-associated with validation via ElementInternals.

Use when users must choose one option from 7+ items. For fewer options, consider `nys-radiobutton`.
For multiple selections, use `nys-checkbox` group instead.
declarations.name: NysSelect

Custom HTML description content below the label.

declarations.description:
`<nys-tabgroup>` is the container for `<nys-tab>` and `<nys-tabpanel>`
elements.

Accepts tabs and panels as flat light-DOM children in any order (interleaved
or grouped). On slot change, children are sorted into dedicated shadow-DOM
containers, ARIA relationships are wired, and the first selected (or first)
tab is activated.

Scroll shadows are rendered on either side of the tab list and toggled via
`ResizeObserver` and a `scroll` listener so they accurately reflect whether
overflow content exists in each direction.

Keyboard navigation follows the
https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tabs/ ARIA Tabs Pattern:
- Arrow keys move focus without changing selection.
- Enter / Space confirm selection on the focused tab.
- Only the selected tab holds a tab stop (`tabindex="0"`); all others are
  removed from the tab sequence (`tabindex="-1"`).
declarations.name: NysTabgroup

slots.description: Accepts `<nys-tab>` and `<nys-tabpanel>` children. Elements are moved into internal shadow-DOM containers on `slotchange`; the slot itself is not rendered visibly.

members.name: id
members.description:
Unique identifier for the tabgroup element.
If not provided, one is auto-generated in `connectedCallback`.
Reflected to the DOM attribute.

members.name: name
members.description:
Accessible label for the tab list (`aria-label` on the inner
`[role="tablist"]`). Should describe the purpose of the tab set
(e.g. `"Account settings"`).

members.name: _tabsEl
members.description:
Reference to the `[role="tablist"]` scroll container in the shadow DOM.
Cached in `firstUpdated` and used by `_updateScrollShadows` to read
scroll position and dimensions.

members.name: _shadowLeft
members.description:
Reference to the left scroll-shadow overlay element.
Receives the `is-visible` class when the tab list is scrolled away from
its leftmost position.

members.name: _shadowRight
members.description:
Reference to the right scroll-shadow overlay element.
Receives the `is-visible` class when overflow content exists to the right
of the current scroll position.

members.name: _resizeObserver
members.description:
`ResizeObserver` instance watching `_tabsEl` for size changes.
Re-evaluates scroll shadow visibility whenever the tab list is resized
(e.g. viewport resize, dynamic tab additions).
Stored so it can be disconnected if needed.

members.name: firstUpdated
members.description:
Called after the element's shadow DOM has been rendered for the first time.

Caches references to the tab list and scroll-shadow elements, performs an
initial scroll-shadow evaluation, and attaches:
- A `scroll` event listener on `_tabsEl` to update shadows on scroll.
- A `ResizeObserver` on `_tabsEl` to update shadows when the container
  is resized.

members.name: _updateScrollShadows
members.description:
Reads the current scroll state of `_tabsEl` and toggles the `is-visible`
class on the left and right shadow overlays accordingly.

- Left shadow is visible when `scrollLeft > 0`.
- Right shadow is visible when `scrollLeft + clientWidth < scrollWidth`
  (i.e. content exists beyond the right edge).

Defined as an arrow function so it can be passed directly as an event
listener without losing `this` context.

members.name: _getTabs
members.description:
Returns all `<nys-tab>` elements currently residing in the shadow-DOM
tabs container, in DOM order.

members.name: _getPanels
members.description:
Returns all `<nys-tabpanel>` elements currently residing in the
shadow-DOM panels container, in DOM order.

members.name: _applySelection

parameters.name: tabs
parameters.description: Ordered array of `<nys-tab>` elements to update.

parameters.name: panels
parameters.description:
Ordered array of `<nys-tabpanel>` elements to update.
Must be the same length as `tabs` for correct pairing.

parameters.name: selectedIndex
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the tab/panel pair to activate.

members.description:
Single source of truth for ARIA wiring, `tabindex`, and panel visibility.

For each index `i`:
- Sets `selected` / removes `selected` attribute on `tabs[i]`.
- Sets `tabIndex` property to `0` (selected) or `-1` (others) on `tabs[i]`.
- Sets `aria-controls` on `tabs[i]` to the `id` of `panels[i]`.
- Sets `aria-labelledby` on `panels[i]` to the `id` of `tabs[i]`.
- Removes `hidden` from `panels[selectedIndex]`; adds it to all others.

Must be called any time the selected tab changes (initial render and
subsequent user interactions).

members.name: _sortChildren

parameters.name: e
parameters.description: The `Event` fired by the `<slot>` element on slot change.

members.description:
Handles `slotchange` on the default slot.

Iterates over all assigned elements and moves each `<nys-tab>` into
`.nys-tabgroup__tabs` and each `<nys-tabpanel>` into
`.nys-tabgroup__panels`, preserving relative order. After sorting,
calls `_applySelection` using the first element that already has a
`selected` attribute, or index `0` if none is found.

members.name: _handleTabSelect
members.description:
Handles the `nys-tab-select` custom event bubbled up from a child
`<nys-tab>`.

Resolves the originating `<nys-tab>` via `composedPath()` (required
because the event crosses shadow DOM boundaries), determines its index
among the current tab list, and delegates to `_applySelection`.

members.name: _handleKeydown
members.description:
Handles `keydown` events on the `[role="tablist"]` container.

Implements the ARIA radio-button keyboard pattern:
- `ArrowRight` — moves focus to the next enabled tab (wraps).
- `ArrowLeft` — moves focus to the previous enabled tab (wraps).

Focus is moved without changing selection; Enter / Space on the newly
focused tab (handled by `<nys-tab>._handleKeydown`) confirm selection.

The currently focused tab is resolved via `composedPath()` because focus
may sit on a shadow-DOM descendant of `<nys-tab>` rather than the host
itself.

Disabled tabs are excluded from navigation and will never receive focus
via arrow keys.

declarations.description:
A single step within `nys-stepper`. Represents one stage in a multi-step process.

Mark as `current` to indicate active progress point. Previous steps become clickable for navigation.
Set `href` for page-based navigation or listen to `nys-step-click` for SPA routing.

## Step States

Understanding the three step states is critical for proper stepper usage:

- **`selected`** - Which step is currently being displayed/viewed. This controls which step's
  content is shown. Defaults to `current` if not set. Cannot be set on a step after `current`.
  Users can click previous steps to change `selected` without changing `current`.

- **`current`** - The furthest step the user has reached. This is the progress boundary.
  Update this as the user completes steps and advances. Steps after `current` are not navigable.
  Only one step should have `current` at a time.

- **`previous`** - Auto-applied by the stepper to all steps before `current`. Do not set manually.
  Steps with `previous` are clickable and allow the user to navigate back.

## Common Patterns

**Initial state:** Set `current` on the first step. `selected` will default to it.
```html
<nys-step label="Step 1" current></nys-step>
<nys-step label="Step 2"></nys-step>
```

**User completed step 1, now on step 2:**
```html
<nys-step label="Step 1"></nys-step>
<nys-step label="Step 2" current></nys-step>
```

**User went back to review step 1 (but progress is still at step 2):**
```html
<nys-step label="Step 1" selected></nys-step>
<nys-step label="Step 2" current></nys-step>
```

declarations.description:
A multi-line text input for collecting longer responses like comments, descriptions, or feedback.
Form-associated with validation support via ElementInternals.

Use for detailed responses needing multiple lines. For single-line input, use `nys-textinput`.
For predefined options, use `nys-select`, `nys-radiobutton`, or `nys-checkbox`.
declarations.name: NysTextarea

slots.description: Custom HTML description content below the label.
slots.name: description

members.name: id
members.description: Unique identifier. Auto-generated if not provided.

members.name: name
members.description: Name for form submission.

members.name: label
members.description: Visible label text. Required for accessibility.

members.name: description
members.description: Helper text below label. Use slot for custom HTML.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Placeholder text. Don't use as label replacement.

members.name: value
members.description: Current textarea value.

members.name: disabled
members.description: Prevents interaction.

members.name: readonly
members.description: Makes textarea read-only but focusable.

members.name: required
members.description: Marks as required. Shows "Required" flag and validates on blur.

members.name: optional
members.description: Shows "Optional" flag. Use when most fields are required.

members.name: tooltip
members.description: Tooltip text shown on hover/focus of info icon.

members.name: inverted
members.description: Adjusts colors for dark backgrounds.

members.name: form
members.description: Form `id` to associate with when textarea is outside form element.

members.name: maxlength
members.description: Maximum character length.

members.name: width
members.description: Textarea width: `sm` (88px), `md` (200px), `lg` (384px), `full` (100%, default).

members.name: rows
members.description: Visible height in lines.

members.name: resize
members.description: Resize behavior: `vertical` (default, user can resize height), `none` (fixed size).

members.name: showError
members.description: Shows error message when true. Set by validation or manually.

members.name: errorMessage
members.description: Error message text. Shown only when `showError` is true.

events.description: Fired on input change. Detail: `{id, value}`.

events.name: nys-focus
events.description: Fired when textarea gains focus.

events.name: nys-blur
events.description: Fired when textarea loses focus. Triggers validation.

events.name: nys-select
events.description: Fired when user selects text. Detail: `{id, value}`.

declarations.description:
A text input for collecting short, single-line data. Supports validation, input masking (tel),
password visibility toggle, and live error messaging. Form-associated via ElementInternals.

Use for names, emails, passwords, phone numbers. For multi-line input, use `nys-textarea` instead.
For predefined options, use `nys-select`, `nys-radiobutton`, or `nys-checkbox`.

members.name: id
members.description: Full YouTube URL — required. Component will not render if invalid.

members.name: titleText
members.description: Title text for the thumbnail of the video

members.name: videourl
members.description: Full YouTube URL — required. Component will not render if invalid.

members.name: size
members.description:
Largest size for the video player.
If not set, size is determined automatically by viewport width.

members.name: starttime
members.description: Time in seconds where playback begins. *

members.name: thumbnail
members.description:
Custom thumbnail image path.
Falls back to YouTube's auto-generated thumbnail if not provided.

members.name: autoplay
members.description: Triggers autoplay when the iframe loads

members.name: disabled
members.description: Prevents the video from being played

members.name: _playerActive
members.description: Tracks whether the user has clicked to load the player

members.name: _announcement
members.description: Screen reader announcement text

members.name: _adPlaying
members.description: Tracks whether an ad is currently playing to suppress false "Video is playing" announcements

members.name: _announceVideoVO
members.description:
Because I need to know if Youtube ADs are playing, I need to call YT's API.
Hence, the YT API setup below. The VO has 2 types of announcements:
- "Advertisement is playing"
- "Video is playing"

YT IFrame Player API: https://developers.google.com/youtube/iframe_api_reference

declarations.description: Get the path to tokens.css file

declarations.name: toCSSVariable
declarations.description:
Transform a JSON token path to CSS variable name

Examples:
- "primitive.color.neutral.900" → "--nys-color-neutral-900"
- "applied.color.text.default" → "--nys-color-text"
- "color.theme.default" → "--nys-color-theme"
- "font.size.md" → "--nys-font-size-md"

declarations.name: aliasToVarRef
declarations.description:
Convert an alias reference to CSS var() syntax

Examples:
- "{primitive.color.neutral.900}" → "var(--nys-color-neutral-900)"
- "{font.size.md}" → "var(--nys-font-size-md)"
